task_description: Write a 4-page maximum report covering paper summary, reproduction gap, ablation findings, failure mode, and honest reflection

full_llm_interaction_log.purpose: Planning the report structure to cover all required sections within 4 pages
full_llm_interaction_log.prompt: Plan a 4-page academic report for Part B that covers: paper summary, reproduction result and gap explanation, two ablation findings, failure mode, honest reflection. How should I allocate page space?
full_llm_interaction_log.how_it_helped: Claude suggested: 0.5 pages paper summary, 0.75 pages reproduction, 1 page ablations (0.5 each), 0.75 pages failure mode, 0.5 pages reflection, 0.5 pages figures/references. This gave me a concrete word budget per section.
full_llm_interaction_log.student_verification: I adjusted the allocation — gave reflection more space (0.75 pages) since it is where I discuss what I actually learned, and compressed the paper summary (0.4 pages) since it mostly summarises Task 1.1 which is already detailed.
full_llm_interaction_log.student_modification: Adjusted page allocation: reduced paper summary, expanded reflection to include discussion of what the SMS spam task revealed about the paper's assumptions in practice.

full_llm_interaction_log.purpose: Drafting the honest reflection section — what the experiments actually revealed about the paper
full_llm_interaction_log.prompt: Help me write a genuine honest reflection (0.75 pages) on what running the reproduction and ablations revealed about Ott et al. 2011 that I did not understand from reading the paper alone.
full_llm_interaction_log.how_it_helped: Claude suggested framing the reflection around three discoveries: (1) the paper's accuracy metric is misleading under imbalance (from Task 3.2), (2) IDF matters more than bigrams in practice (from ablations), (3) SMS spam being 'easier' actually validates the paper's hardest design choice (the deceptive task construction). I used these as the three pillars of the reflection but wrote the content myself.
full_llm_interaction_log.student_verification: The reflection is my own writing. I wrote it after completing all experiments and drawing on my actual experience running the code, seeing the F1 collapse, and comparing the ablation drops.
full_llm_interaction_log.student_modification: N/A — the reflection section was written entirely by me. Claude's contribution was suggesting the three-pillar structure, but every sentence in the final text is my own.

full_llm_interaction_log.purpose: Generating the report PDF with proper academic formatting
full_llm_interaction_log.prompt: Generate a 4-page academic report PDF for Part B using the content from all task notebooks. Include all required sections, figures from results/, and proper citations to Ott et al. 2011.
full_llm_interaction_log.how_it_helped: Claude produced the report.pdf using a Python PDF generation script with all sections, embedded result figures, and formatted citations. This saved significant time on PDF layout and formatting.
full_llm_interaction_log.student_verification: I reviewed every section of the generated PDF against my notebooks to ensure consistency. I corrected the ablation results table (Claude's draft had slightly wrong fold numbers) and rewrote the conclusion paragraph to match my actual reflection notes.
full_llm_interaction_log.student_modification: Corrected ablation results table values to match exact fold-level outputs. Rewrote the conclusion/reflection paragraph entirely in my own voice. Added a note in the reproduction section acknowledging that the higher-than-paper accuracy is expected rather than suspicious.

top_5_prompts.prompt_summary: Generate report PDF with all sections, embedded figures, and citations
top_5_prompts.why_most_useful: Produced the full report layout; I focused on content accuracy and rewrote the reflection in my own voice.

top_5_prompts.prompt_summary: Three-pillar structure for honest reflection on what experiments revealed
top_5_prompts.why_most_useful: Suggested a framing that matched my actual experimental findings — I used it as scaffolding for writing my own reflection.

top_5_prompts.prompt_summary: 4-page report structure with page allocation per section
top_5_prompts.why_most_useful: Prevented me from over-expanding early sections and running out of space for the reflection, which carries the most analytical weight.

top_5_prompts.prompt_summary: Correct ablation table and rewrite conclusion to match actual results
top_5_prompts.why_most_useful: My own corrections ensured the report is accurate and matches the notebook outputs exactly.

top_5_prompts.prompt_summary: Discovery that IDF matters more than bigrams — framing for ablation section
top_5_prompts.why_most_useful: This cross-ablation comparative insight became the headline finding of the ablation section in the report.

student_declaration.statement: I declare that this JSON file contains a complete and honest record of my LLM usage for Task 4.1 of Part B.
